title: ambrosia camphorata
intro: Ambrosia camphorata, also known as Gum Plant or Gum Resin Tree, is a shrub native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico, producing a sticky, fragrant resin that resembles gum. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ambrosia camphorata include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and its potential to reduce pain and inflammation. In herbal preparations, the resin is often used to make topical ointments, salves, and tinctures, as well as being added to teas and infusions for its medicinal properties.
